questions: transfer learning ar fine tuning ki same?
answers: Transfer learning is when a model trained on one task is reused as the starting point for a different task. For example, a model trained on ImageNet can be adapted to detect diseases in X-rays.
Transfer learning holo ekta task e shekha knowledge onno task e apply kora. Eta time ar data bachay karon model ke shuru theke shuru korte hoy na.
In transfer learning, knowledge from a pre-trained model is transferred to a new problem. It works because early layers learn general features that are useful across many tasks.
Transfer learning saves massive computation and data requirements. Instead of training from scratch, you leverage existing models like ResNet, BERT, or GPT and adapt them for your specific needs.
